items.prompt: Which statement best describes the Internet of Things in this part?
choices: Only social media APIs with no physical devices
A network of physical devices that connect and exchange data
A single offline spreadsheet of sensor readings
items.explain: IoT networks physical devices that collect, share, and analyze data with limited human intervention.

items.prompt: IoT typically combines sensing devices, connectivity, and edge or cloud processing.
items.explain: Section 9.3.1 structures IoT around things, connectivity, and data processing analytics.

items.prompt: Example 9.14 (industrial machine monitoring) emphasizes devices that:
choices: Monitor performance, detect faults, and predict maintenance
items.explain: Industrial machines sense their own state to support fault detection and predictive maintenance.

items.prompt: Name one connectivity option suitable for long-distance, low-payload agricultural sensors.
items.rubric: Accept LPWAN / low-power wide-area networks, or cellular when justified; LPWAN is the textbook fit.
items.sample_answer: Low-power wide-area networks (LPWAN) are used for small amounts of data over long distances, such as agricultural sensors.

items.prompt: Example 9.16 argues that continuous soil sensing can support timelier irrigation decisions than infrequent manual tests alone.
items.explain: Continuous agricultural streams enable timely decisions that sparse manual soil tests cannot match.
